wrapUp: Four former champions have reached the semi-finals for the first time since 1990, with France and Spain renewing their rivalry in Dallas on 14 July and England facing Argentina in Atlanta a day later. Kylian Mbappe and Aurelien Tchouameni trained through France's quarter-final week while William Saliba and Dayot Upamecano were managed ahead of the Spain tie, and Thomas Tuchel has scaled back England's workload as Declan Rice recovers from illness with Jarell Quansah still suspended. Lionel Messi and Jude Bellingham head into a first competitive England-Argentina meeting since 2002, while Mikel Merino has scored late winners in Spain's last two knockouts. Each story below links to the specific report it is drawn from; squad and fitness details reflect the latest available reporting as of 12 July 2026.

sections.heading: Injuries and fitness doubts
sections.description: Players ruled out, late replacements and day-to-day concerns that could change starting line-ups and squad depth.

stories.headline: Tuchel eases training as Rice recovers from illness
stories.summary: Thomas Tuchel said Declan Rice spent most of the three days before England's quarter-final against Norway in bed with sickness and was substituted at half-time in Miami as the Three Lions came from behind to win 2-1 after extra time. BBC Sport reported Tuchel will drop training intensity at England's Kansas base before Wednesday's Atlanta semi-final against Argentina, with Ezri Konsa also struggling with cramps in the Florida heat.

stories.headline: Mbappe and Tchouameni on course for Dallas semi-final
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappe went down with 20 minutes left in France's 2-0 win over Morocco and had ice applied to his right foot, but L'Equipe reported the camp treated it as only a knock and he trained normally before the Spain tie. Aurelien Tchouameni missed the quarter-final with an adductor problem sustained on the eve of the Paraguay tie but returned to training in Boston, with Didier Deschamps expected to restore him ahead of Manu Kone in midfield for Tuesday's rematch with La Roja.

stories.headline: Saliba and Upamecano miss France session before Spain tie
stories.summary: William Saliba and Dayot Upamecano were absent from France's Saturday training at Bentley University three days before the Dallas semi-final against Spain, with Get French Football News reporting both are nevertheless expected to be available. Saliba's back issue has been managed since the start of the tournament, while Upamecano was rested for foot pain after starting the 2-0 quarter-final win over Morocco. Kylian Mbappe, who left that match with an ankle knock, and Aurelien Tchouameni, returning from a thigh problem, both trained as normal.

stories.headline: Quansah ban confirmed for semi-final against Argentina
stories.summary: FIFA imposed a two-match suspension on Jarell Quansah after his red card against Mexico, meaning he serves the second game of the ban in Atlanta and will not face Argentina unless the ruling is overturned. Metro reported the FA did not appeal after FIFA's unprecedented suspension of Folarin Balogun's ban, leaving Thomas Tuchel to continue rotating Djed Spence and John Stones at right-back through the semi-finals.

sections.heading: Squad selection and tactical reshuffles
sections.description: Omissions, late call-ups and manager decisions that reveal how teams plan to navigate the expanded format.

stories.headline: Alvarez screamer sends Argentina into semi-finals
stories.summary: Julian Alvarez curled a 25-yard strike into the top corner in the 112th minute to break a stubborn Swiss defence after Breel Embolo's controversial second yellow for simulation, and Lautaro Martinez added a third in stoppage time of extra time for a 3-1 win in Kansas City. FIFA's match report noted Alexis Mac Allister had headed in a Lionel Messi corner for an early lead, and Argentina will face England in Atlanta on 15 July for a place in the final.

stories.headline: Bellingham double sends England past Norway in extra time
stories.summary: Jude Bellingham equalised in first-half stoppage time and scored again three minutes into extra time after Orjan Nyland spilled Morgan Rogers' shot, overturning Andreas Schjelderup's opener as England won 2-1 in Miami. FIFA's match report noted Harry Kane had a goal ruled out for offside and Erling Haaland was substituted at the break of extra time, setting up a semi-final with Argentina that will be shown live on the BBC.

stories.headline: Merino strikes late again as Spain beat Belgium
stories.summary: Mikel Merino scored in the 88th minute after Pau Cubarsi's long-range effort was parried by Senne Lammens, giving Spain a 2-1 quarter-final win in Los Angeles and their first World Cup semi-final since 2010. France 24 reported Fabian Ruiz had opened the scoring before Charles De Ketelaere headed Belgium level with the first goal Spain had conceded in the tournament, teeing up a Dallas showdown with France on Bastille Day.

stories.headline: Mbappe and Dembele send France past Morocco
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappe converted a 60th-minute penalty rebound and Ousmane Dembele added a second six minutes later as France beat Morocco 2-0 in Foxborough, matching the scoreline from their 2022 semi-final meeting. ESPN reported Mbappe's strike was his eighth of the tournament and moved him to 20 World Cup career goals, one behind Lionel Messi, with Les Bleus now targeting a third consecutive World Cup final in Dallas.

sections.heading: Disruption and preparation issues
sections.description: Travel, visa and logistical problems that could affect match readiness rather than just headlines.

stories.headline: Embolo dismissal reshapes Argentina quarter-final
stories.summary: Breel Embolo was sent off in the 72nd minute of Argentina's quarter-final against Switzerland after a VAR review upgraded a simulation caution to a second yellow, the first use of mistaken-identity protocol at this World Cup. Al Jazeera reported Switzerland had levelled through Dan Ndoye before the red card and then defended for nearly 50 minutes with 10 men until Alvarez's extra-time winner, leaving Argentina to face England with another 30 minutes in their legs.

stories.headline: England cut training load before Atlanta semi-final
stories.summary: England returned to their Kansas base after 120 minutes in Miami's heat and humidity and have three days to prepare for Argentina in Atlanta, with Tuchel promising to reduce training intensity so Rice and other walking wounded can recover. Yahoo Sports reported the squad face another flight before matchday minus two on Monday, limiting formal preparation time for a semi-final Thomas Tuchel admitted England were lucky to reach.

stories.headline: France fly to Dallas after penultimate Boston session
stories.summary: France rested on Friday after beating Morocco, trained in Waltham on Saturday without Saliba and Upamecano, and were due to leave their Boston base on Sunday afternoon for the four-hour flight to Texas. Get French Football News reported media duties from Monday morning ruled out a same-day flight, compressing the build-up to a Bastille Day semi-final at Dallas Stadium against a Spain side they have beaten in their last two tournament meetings.

sections.heading: Analysis and tournament outlook
sections.description: Deeper previews on favourites, group dynamics and how the 48-team format changes the path to the final.

stories.headline: Four former champions reach semi-finals together
stories.summary: Argentina, England, France and Spain are the last four teams standing, the first time four past winners have shared a World Cup semi-final line-up since Italy 1990. Al Jazeera reported Opta now makes France 34 percent title favourites ahead of Spain, England and Argentina, with Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappe tied on eight goals at the head of the Golden Boot race and Jude Bellingham on six after his Norway brace.

stories.headline: Mbappe and Yamal renew rivalry in Dallas shootout
stories.summary: France and Spain meet for the third time in two years after La Roja beat Les Bleus at Euro 2024 and in the 2025 Nations League, with Lamine Yamal telling reporters France should fear Spain rather than the reverse. Al Jazeera noted Opta's model gives France a 42.1 percent chance of winning in regulation on 14 July, while Spain have conceded only once in six matches and Mikel Merino has scored late winners in both knockout ties.

stories.headline: Messi and Bellingham head into historic semi-final
stories.summary: England and Argentina have not met at a World Cup since David Beckham's red card and Michael Owen's opener in Sapporo in 2002, but BBC Sport noted Lionel Messi is set for his first competitive meeting with the Three Lions in Atlanta on 15 July. Argentina have needed extra time in two of three knockout matches, while England also went to 120 minutes against Norway, so both semi-finalists arrive with similar fatigue profiles before a rematch of the 1986 Hand of God tie.

stories.headline: Tuchel wants sharper England before Argentina test
stories.summary: Thomas Tuchel praised his side's spirit after the Norway win but told BBC Sport he was not happy with a sloppy, tactically loose performance in Miami, highlighting failures to exploit Norway's 4-5-1 block through wide rotations. England's tactics correspondent noted Tuchel's ideal patterns of short passing to draw pressure before switching play appeared only in flashes, leaving Bellingham's instinct in transitional moments as the decisive factor again.
